title: Credentials, Signing, Retries, Timeouts, and Client Resilience · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A delivery team incrementally builds bounded S3 request and retry state machine, changes one file or failure, and keeps earlier HTTP, Go, accessibility, security, and resource-budget invariants active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of bounded S3 request and retry state machine while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use short-lived credential providers with explicit workload identity, refresh ownership, least privilege, and redaction.
Explain SigV4 canonical request, region, service, signed headers, payload mode, clock, and credential scope without hand-building production signatures.
Compose request, attempt, transfer, and shutdown deadlines and propagate cancellation through body and goroutine ownership.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Credential Provider Chain
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go file-delivery team handles case 3953 during a private media release. Build deterministic Go evidence to classify S3 outcomes and bound retries while preserving write ambiguity; do not treat an ETag, CORS result, emulator, or successful SDK call as stronger proof than it is; then change region, range, or signed header and explain the exact failure boundary. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Credential Provider Chain.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use short-lived credential providers with explicit workload identity, refresh ownership, least privilege, and redaction. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Credential Provider Chain, keep Credential Provider Chain connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.
